title: pneumonanthe ochroleuca
intro: Pneumonanthe ochroleuca, also known as Marsh Marigold, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the buttercup family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to aid in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The health benefits of Pneumonanthe ochroleuca include its ability to stimulate the immune system, reduce fever, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues such as coughs and colds. The plant is typically prepared as an herbal tea or tincture, which can be used internally to promote overall health and well-being.
